message:
fix(pager,btree,wal,core): concurrent freelist hole reclamation, partial index integrity, and WAL lifecycle hardening

Pager -- concurrent transaction rollback (explicit and Drop) now returns
EOF-allocated pages to the in-memory freelist unconditionally instead of
silently discarding pages beyond db_size.  This eliminates "Page N: never
used" corruption that occurred when a later commit grew past the holes.
Extracted shared `return_pages_to_freelist` helper and added three new
tests (rollback, savepoint rollback, Drop path).  RESTART/TRUNCATE
checkpoint modes are conservatively downgraded to FULL until the pager
tracks external reader end marks, preventing premature WAL reset.

Pager traits -- added `MemoryMockMvccPager` and `MemoryMockTransaction`
for cross-crate tests that need durable in-memory page writes (the
existing `MockMvccPager`/`MockTransaction` always returns zeroed pages).

B-tree cursor -- added `reload_page_fresh` to bypass stale stack-entry
cache after in-place writes; used in insert, delete, and
replace_interior_cell paths.  Fixed interior index delete to walk to the
duplicate successor via `advance_next` instead of re-seeking (which
would land on the freshly-written separator).  Fixed left-child pointer
encoding in interior cells to use big-endian u32 instead of varint.
Fixed `compare_index_key_bytes` signature to accept `Option<&[T]>`
instead of `&Option<Vec<T>>`.  Added tests for interior separator
delete and repeated root overflow orphan detection.

WAL checkpoint executor -- extracted `apply_checkpoint_post_action` and
ensured the post-action (reset/truncate) fires even when
frames_to_backfill is zero (already fully backfilled).  Added tests for
RESTART and TRUNCATE on pre-backfilled WAL.

Connection -- WAL installation now checks file size before attempting
parse, uses pager's actual page_size instead of hardcoded DEFAULT, and
propagates WalCorrupt errors instead of silently swallowing them.
integrity_check now evaluates partial index WHERE predicates so rows
excluded by the predicate are not falsely flagged as missing from the
index.  Added extensive Beads-workload compatibility tests: single-
connection and multi-threaded concurrent index churn with partial
indexes, prepared-statement insert after reopen, and WAL page-size
propagation.

VDBE engine -- hoisted DESC flag and collation registry lookups above
the cursor borrow to avoid holding mutable + immutable borrows
simultaneously.

Datetime -- narrowed wildcard error pattern to `Err(())` for clippy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
